fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Maryland Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Maryland - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity maryland, how to transfer dietitian license to maryland, dietitian compact maryland, maryland dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Board of Dietetic Practice
board.mailing_address:
Maryland Board of Dietetic Practice
4201 Patterson Avenue
Baltimore, MD 21215-2299

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years + 30 CPE hours (incl. implicit bias)
quick_facts.ce_requirement: 30 hours per renewal cycle (incl. implicit bias)

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian Nutritionist (LDN)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice dietetics and medical nutrition therapy in Maryland. Requires current CDR registration as an RD/RDN or CNS certification.

steps.title: Create Online Account
steps.description: Navigate to the Maryland Professional Online Licensing System at licensing.opl.health.maryland.gov/ and click "New User? Create an Account" to register with your email address and create a password.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your newly created account, select the "Dietitian/Nutritionist" license application, and accurately complete all required sections of the online form.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload all necessary supporting documents, which typically include official transcripts, verification of supervised practice hours, and Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) registration, directly through the Maryland Professional Online Licensing System.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable application fee of $150 via credit card or other accepted payment methods through the online licensing system to finalize your submission.

steps.title: Board Review
steps.description: The Maryland Board of Dietetic Practice will review your completed application and all submitted documentation for compliance with state licensure requirements.

steps.title: Receive Licensure Notification
steps.description: Upon approval, you will receive notification of your issued license through the Maryland Professional Online Licensing System, and your license will be active.

documents.detail: From an accredited college or university, may be emailed to Lenelle.Cooper@maryland.gov or mailed to the Board.
Your Commission on Dietetic Registration (RD/RDN) or Board for Certification of Nutrition Specialists (CNS) number for direct verification.
Paper applications require notarized signature page.
May be required at the Board's discretion during the application process.

provisional_text: Maryland does not offer a provisional or temporary license for dietitians. A current, valid CDR registration (RD/RDN) or CNS certification is required before a license can be issued.
reciprocity_text: Maryland requires state-specific licensure to practice, even if you are licensed in another state. However, the process is streamlined for Registered Dietitians who already hold CDR registration. Out-of-state licensees apply through the standard application process and can transfer their CDR registration. Contact the Board directly for specific reciprocity guidance.

faqs.question: How much does a Maryland dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial combined application and licensure fee is $300 (a non-refundable one-time payment). Biennial renewal costs $250, which may include a $26 MHCC user fee.

faqs.question: Which Maryland agency regulates dietitians?
faqs.answer: The Board of Dietetic Practice, which operates under the Maryland Department of Health (MDH), is the regulatory body for dietitian licensure in Maryland.

faqs.question: What are the continuing education requirements for Maryland dietitians?
faqs.answer: Licensees must complete a minimum of 30 hours of continuing professional education (CPE) during each two-year renewal cycle. This must include a one-time implicit bias CEU, which can count toward the 30-hour total. The Board may conduct random audits.

faqs.question: Does Maryland have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Maryland. To work in Maryland, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Maryland?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Maryland while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

reciprocity.notes: Maryland requires state-specific licensure to practice, even if you are licensed in another state. However, the process is streamlined for Registered Dietitians who already hold CDR registration. Out-of-state licensees apply through the standard application process and can transfer their CDR registration. Contact the Board directly for specific reciprocity guidance.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Maryland
hero.dek: To apply for a Dietitian or Nutritionist license in Maryland, visit the Maryland Professional Online Licensing System (also known as the OneStop Portal) and create an account to submit your application online.

official_links.registration_steps: To create an account and start your application, navigate to the Maryland Professional Online Licensing System. Click on "New User? Create an Account" and follow the prompts to register with a valid email and create a password. Once logged in, you can select the application for Dietitian/Nutritionist.
